findings.description: All 20 regular tile files were visually inspected. The rerun dimension repair is successful: each listed regular tile is 128x128, centered enough for board use, and the subjects remain mutually distinguishable at target size.
findings.repair_suggestion: No dimension repair needed for regular tiles.

findings.description: Several regular tiles contain visible baked-in annotation text such as pixel-size notes or quality notes. The text is visible in the final 128x128 asset and would appear on the game board.
findings.repair_suggestion: Regenerate or clean these tiles from source art without any measurement labels, prompt notes, signatures, or quality annotations.

findings.description: All five special tactical tiles were visually inspected at 128x128. The required markers are readable: wave rings, chain links, a single directional arrow, bidirectional swap arrows, and sequential cascade dots. They are visually distinct from the regular tile subjects.
findings.repair_suggestion: No visual repair needed for the special tactical tile rerun.

findings.description: Ritsu's regenerated expression set now keeps the same dark disheveled hair, rolled light blue-grey shirt, lanky build, and recognizable identity across all five emotions.

Umeko still does not read as the same ~55-year-old woman across all five emotions. The bamboo-green apron and updo/bun are present, but the face shape, age, and body scale drift: default is a mature broad-faced woman with stronger age lines, thinking and react_jump become much younger and smaller, and the set does not hold one consistent mature facial structure.
findings.repair_suggestion: Regenerate Umeko as one locked character model across all emotions, preserving the same ~55-year-old face shape, laugh lines, bun silhouette, bamboo-green apron, and body scale while changing only the emotion pose.

findings.description: Multiple expression assets have baked-in text, punctuation, symbols, or signature marks. This violates the no text baked into artwork requirement and makes the assets unsuitable for direct in-game use.
findings.repair_suggestion: Regenerate or clean the affected expression PNGs with no punctuation, signatures, captions, labels, or Japanese text in the image.

findings.description: Some emotions are not clearly distinct. Gicho's happy and react_jump both read mainly as closed-eye smiling cat busts, and Kenzo's react_jump lacks the expected energetic full-body reaction compared with his default pose.
findings.repair_suggestion: Regenerate react_jump variants with stronger full-body action, raised limbs, motion, and more intensity than happy or surprised.

findings.description: The rerun target for Hinata is visually improved. The happy portrait now shows brown hair tied back, sakura-pink clothing accents, and a worn notebook, so it reads as Hinata rather than the previous wrong-character version.
findings.repair_suggestion: No Hinata identity repair needed, though crop/style normalization with the portrait set would still help.

findings.description: Ritsu's happy portrait partially matches the requested broad markers with dark disheveled hair and a blue-grey shirt, but it still does not match the default portrait identity. The default is a simplified chibi portrait with a rounder face, droopy eyes, flatter parted hair, and a pale blue-grey shirt; the happy portrait is a more realistic adult bust with a longer face, different eye/cheek structure, deeper open collar, and different rendering style.
findings.repair_suggestion: Regenerate char_ritsu_portrait_happy directly from the default portrait identity, preserving the same face structure, eye shape, hair silhouette, shirt cut/color, crop, and watercolor style while changing only the expression.

findings.description: Fuyuko's 512x512 react_jump portrait has a noticeably different composition from the default portrait: it includes a scenic background and Japanese text, making it feel like an illustration panel instead of a neutral dialogue portrait.
findings.repair_suggestion: Regenerate Fuyuko react_jump as a neutral face-and-shoulders portrait with no scene background and no text, matching the default portrait crop and style.

findings.description: The portrait dimension set is still inconsistent. Ritsu happy is 256x256 despite the batch doc listing 1024x1024, repaired Hinata happy is 1254x1254 rather than the listed 512x512, Fuyuko react_jump remains 512x512, and most other portraits are 1024x1024.
findings.repair_suggestion: Normalize portrait export dimensions after visual fixes so all dialogue portraits share one documented source size.

findings.description: The idle loop does not close cleanly. idle_f04 is visibly smaller, has a different face/collar proportion, and would pop when followed by idle_f01.
findings.repair_suggestion: Rebuild idle_f04 directly from idle_f01 with only a subtle return-to-neutral breathing offset, matching scale, head size, collar, and body position.

findings.description: Gicho's orange tabby identity is not locked across the 15 animation frames. Head size, body width, collar thickness, face shape, and overall scale shift between frames and sets.
findings.repair_suggestion: Use one canonical Gicho base drawing and deform it frame-to-frame instead of regenerating independent cat images.

findings.description: The wave and surprised sequences show abrupt pose/scale discontinuities. The wave motion is present, but wave_f04 changes into a different lowered-paw pose and surprised_f03 de-escalates after the peak rather than continuing the shock progression.
findings.repair_suggestion: Rework the affected sequences as true in-between frames from a single rig or base pose, preserving scale and center point.

findings.description: The representative assets do not fully feel like one coherent game world. The tiles are muted watercolor icons, Ritsu is a tiny full-body chibi, Hinata reads cleaner anime, Kenzo reads storybook portrait, and Gicho reads more naturalistic animal illustration. Palette warmth overlaps, but rendering style and proportion language are inconsistent.
findings.repair_suggestion: Define one cross-family style target and re-normalize characters/portraits/animation toward the same watercolor texture, outline treatment, chibi proportion, and saturation level used by the accepted tile direction.

batch_summaries.notes: Rerun dimension repair passed for all 20 regular tiles, but several tiles contain visible baked-in annotation text.
All five special tile markers are readable at 128x128 and distinct from regular tiles.
Ritsu expression identity repair passed, but Umeko still fails the locked ~55yo identity check across emotions; several images include baked text/symbols; some react_jump emotions are ambiguous.
Hinata happy now matches Hinata, but Ritsu happy only matches broad hair/shirt markers and still fails default-portrait face/style continuity; Fuyuko react_jump remains compositionally inconsistent.
Gicho animation rerun still fails identity consistency and idle loop closure; wave motion exists but sequence continuity is unstable.
Representative assets remain visually fragmented across tile, expression, portrait, and animation families.
